medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presenting with chest pressure. She was at her cardiology appointment today, and described few weeks of chest pressure with exertion, improves with rest. Chest pressure lasts a few minutes and resolves on its own. Not associated with diaphoresis, shortness of breath, arm tingling, abdominal pain. EKG at the office showed concern for ischemia with q waves in III, V1-V3, ST elevations in V1-V4 and TWI in V5-V6. She denies fever, chills, night sweats, or recent surgery. In the ED, initial vitals were: 97.4, HR 57, BP 154/79. Labs showed Cr 6.9; Trop 0.1. Decision was made to admit for rule out ACS.
medical_history.past_medical_history: CAD; CABG; DM type I c/b peripheral neuropathy, gastroparesis, ESRD; ESRD previously PD, tunneled IJ; HTN; Anemia of chronic disease; h/o enterococcal peritonitis; h/o frequent DKA; Hypothyroidism; Diastolic heart failure; Depression; Anxiety; Osteopenia; Bulimia Eating disorder; h/o bacterial overgrowth; h/o vertebral fracture; h/o stress fracture in right metatarsal; h/o pericarditis; s/p cataract surgery.
medical_history.family_history: Grandfather (paternal) - died of myocardial infarction in his early age; Mother - died of ovarian cancer; Father - history of hypertension.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: 99.2, 106/78, HR 66, RR 18, 99% RA. Young woman, comfortable, pleasant, NAD.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neuro: moving all extremities grossly. Cardiovascular: regular rhythm, no m/r/g. Lungs: faint bibasilar crackles, no wheezes, ronchi. Abdomen: soft, NT/ND.
